commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh He asked water, and she gave him milk; she brought forth butter in a lordly dish. Hebrew sephel; whence the sympule of the Lydians, Tuscans, and Romans, was probably derived, denoting a bowl or jug with a handle, designed for libations. They were formerly made of potter's ware, fictilibus prolibatur sympuciis, or sympulis. (Pliny, xxxv. ) "Aut quis Sympuvium ridere Numæ, nigrumve catinum Aut vaticanas fragiles de monte patellas Ausus erat. "(Juvenal, Sat.
